ruleset.name: Opinionated Rules
ruleset.description:
Opt-in checks that are deliberately NOT part of the default `go` ruleset because they conflict with idiomatic Go. They come straight from PHPMD's object-oriented, PHP-flavoured heritage, and flag patterns that are perfectly normal — often unavoidable — in Go.
You can run these if you want a stricter, more opinionated style, but they are not part of "idiomatic Go":
* CleanCode/ElseExpression flags every `else` branch. Go uses `else` freely; early returns are encouraged but not required, and `gofmt`/`go vet`/golint do not object to `else`. * CleanCode/BooleanArgumentFlag flags every boolean parameter. Go's own standard library is full of them (e.g. strconv, io, signal), so this is an OO Single-Responsibility opinion rather than a Go convention. * UnusedCode/UnusedFormalParameter flags unread parameters. In Go, parameters are routinely kept to satisfy an interface, an http.HandlerFunc signature, sort.Interface, or a callback shape — the compiler deliberately allows unused parameters for exactly this reason. * Design/GlobalVariable flags mutable package-level variables. Global mutable state hurts testability and concurrency safety, but some package-level variables are idiomatic in Go (sentinel errors, compiled regexps, registries), so this is left out of the default `go` ruleset.
Run with: messgo <paths> <format> opinionated Or combine: messgo <paths> <format> go,opinionated
